definitions.definition: Microeconomics is a branch of economics that studies the behaviour and decision-making of individual economic units such as households, firms, and markets.
definitions.example: For example, it studies how households allocate their income between different goods and services.

definitions.definition: Macroeconomics is a branch of economics that studies the behaviour and decision-making of the economy as a whole, focusing on variables such as inflation, unemployment, and GDP.
Scarcity refers to the basic problem of economics, which is the unavailability of unlimited resources to satisfy the unlimited wants of the people.
Opportunity cost is the value of the next best alternative that is given up when a choice is made.
definitions.example: For example, if a student chooses to study economics, the opportunity cost is the time that could have been spent studying other subjects.

definitions.definition: An economic system is a network of institutions which provide for the production, distribution and exchange of goods and services.
A traditional economy is an economy that relies heavily on customs, habits and conventions.
A market economy is an economic system in which the production, distribution and exchange of goods and services are determined by the forces of demand and supply in a market.
A command economy is an economic system in which the production, distribution and exchange of goods and services are controlled and directed by the government.
A consumer is an economic unit that buys goods and services for personal consumption.
A producer is an economic unit that produces goods and services for sale.
A firm is an economic unit that produces a product or service with the intention of selling it for profit.
A market is a place where buyers and sellers interact to exchange goods and services.
Demand is the quantity of a good or service that consumers are willing and able to buy at a given price level.
Supply is the quantity of a good or service that producers are willing and able to sell at a given price level.
Price mechanism is a system of self-adjusting prices which helps in allocation of resources.
Equilibrium is a state in which the quantity of a commodity that suppliers are willing and able to sell equals the quantity that buyers are willing and able to buy.
Short-run refers to a period of time in which some of the factors of production are fixed.
Long-run refers to a period of time in which all the factors of production are variable.
